metadata.name: Yudhishthira
metadata.primary_role: Eldest Pandava / King of Indraprastha / Son of Dharma / Embodiment of truth and righteousness
metadata.ethnicity_cultural_context: Ancient Indian (Kuru Dynasty, Hastinapur/Indraprastha, son of Kunti and Dharma/Yama)
metadata.divine_origin: Son of Dharma (Yama, God of Justice and Death) — inherits absolute commitment to truth and righteousness

timelines.context_description: Young Yudhishthira as the eldest Pandava prince, known for his unwavering truthfulness and calm demeanor.

physical_appearance.skin_tone: Fair wheat-golden complexion with a serene luminous quality, radiant and calm
physical_appearance.height: Tall and dignified, carries himself with measured royal grace
physical_appearance.build: Lean and regal rather than heavily muscular, the build of a thinker-king, upright noble posture
physical_appearance.facial_features: Calm serene face with large thoughtful eyes radiating moral clarity, high noble forehead, gentle thin lips that never lie, straight nose, expression of perpetual contemplation, faint worry lines from the burden of righteousness
physical_appearance.hair: Neat black hair in a modest royal style, well-groomed and orderly
physical_appearance.distinguishing_marks: His presence radiates calm — people feel compelled to speak truth around him, his aura of dharma is almost physical

wardrobe_and_adornments.clothing: White and gold royal robes — pristine and orderly, befitting the eldest prince, white silk dhoti with minimal gold border
wardrobe_and_adornments.jewelry: Modest gold crown of an heir-prince, simple gold chain, sacred thread prominently displayed, minimal ornamentation compared to more flashy brothers
wardrobe_and_adornments.weapons_carried: A ceremonial spear and a long straight sword — he favors the spear in combat, also a deadly mace fighter (taught by Bhima)

audio_and_voice_characteristics.tone: Calm, measured, deeply thoughtful, speaks with moral authority that makes others pause, never raises his voice in anger
audio_and_voice_characteristics.pitch: Smooth steady baritone — calm, measured, unhurried, the most reassuring voice among the Pandavas
audio_and_voice_characteristics.speech_style: Speaks in complete careful sentences, never exaggerates, weighs every word for truthfulness, uses philosophical language, addresses all with respect regardless of status
audio_and_voice_characteristics.emotional_baseline: Serene moral certainty mixed with the heavy burden of always doing the right thing even when it hurts

cinematic_generation_prompts.visual_prompt: Cinematic portrait of Prince Yudhishthira, age 20, eldest Pandava. Fair wheat-golden complexion, serene calm face, large thoughtful moral eyes, high forehead. Lean regal build in pristine white and gold royal robes. Modest gold crown. Standing in the court of Hastinapur with brothers behind him, expression of calm moral authority. Grand palace interior. 3D Pixar style, warm calm lighting, photorealistic, 8k.
cinematic_generation_prompts.audio_prompt: A calm, measured male baritone voice. Smooth, unhurried, radiating moral authority. Never raises in anger. Age approximately 20 years.

timelines.context_description: Yudhishthira at Kurukshetra — the most reluctant warrior on the field, forced to fight for dharma despite wanting peace.

physical_appearance.skin_tone: Weathered golden complexion, lined with the strain of 13 years of exile and moral agony of the coming war
physical_appearance.height: Still tall and dignified but visibly aged by suffering, shoulders slightly bowed by moral weight
physical_appearance.build: Still lean but now hardened by exile, the gauntness of a man who carries impossible moral burdens, lined and tired
physical_appearance.facial_features: Deeply troubled face — eyes that show the agony of a righteous man forced to kill his own kin and teachers, deep lines of moral suffering, a face that has aged beyond its years from the weight of dharmic choices
physical_appearance.hair: Grey-streaked hair under a simple war crown, thinner than his youth
physical_appearance.distinguishing_marks: The permanent expression of moral suffering — even on the battlefield he looks like a man attending a funeral, his only lie in the entire epic ('Ashwatthama is dead — the elephant') haunts his face

wardrobe_and_adornments.clothing: Simple white armor with gold trim — the least ornate of the Pandava warriors, a white and gold battle cape
wardrobe_and_adornments.jewelry: A simple war crown, his ceremonial chain of dharma, practical battle gear — no ostentation
wardrobe_and_adornments.weapons_carried: His spear (his primary weapon at Kurukshetra), a long sword, a mace — fights competently but without Arjuna's flair or Bhima's fury

audio_and_voice_characteristics.tone: Agonized, conflicted, the voice of a man at war with war itself, profound sadness mixed with duty
audio_and_voice_characteristics.pitch: Deep weary baritone — tired and carrying the weight of kinslaying, steady but laden with sorrow
audio_and_voice_characteristics.speech_style: Speaks of duty and dharma even as he hates the necessity, addresses fallen enemies with respect and grief, his final dharmic declaration after the war is hollow — victory feels like defeat
audio_and_voice_characteristics.emotional_baseline: Profound moral anguish, reluctant warrior's duty, the devastating sadness of a man who won the war but lost his peace

cinematic_generation_prompts.visual_prompt: Solemn cinematic shot of Yudhishthira at Kurukshetra, age 48. Weathered golden face deeply lined with moral suffering. Grey-streaked hair under simple war crown. Simple white and gold armor. Standing on the battlefield with his spear, looking at the carnage with profound grief. Dead warriors around him. Grey overcast sky. 3D Pixar style, somber dramatic lighting, photorealistic, 8k.
cinematic_generation_prompts.audio_prompt: A deep, weary male baritone laden with sorrow and moral anguish. The reluctant voice of duty. Age approximately 48 years.
